content: I'm so excited for my upcoming documentary about chess. I've been reading a lot of books on the subject to get some inspiration.
That sounds like a great project. What drew you to the topic of chess?
I've always been fascinated by the strategic aspect of the game. I've also been playing Brazilian jiu-jitsu for a few years now, and I see a lot of similarities between the two.
That's really interesting. I wouldn't have thought to compare chess and Brazilian jiu-jitsu. Are you planning on featuring any notable chess players in your documentary?
Yeah, I'm actually interviewing a few grandmasters, including my friend, Maria, who's a chess champion from Portugal.
That's great. It's always good to have expert insights. What made you decide to become a documentary filmmaker?

I've always been interested in storytelling, and I found that documentary filmmaking was a great way to combine that with my love of learning. I actually got my start making short films about origami.
Origami is a really unique topic. I can see how that would translate to documentary filmmaking. What do you like to do in your free time besides filmmaking and Brazilian jiu-jitsu?
I also enjoy reading sci-fi novels and playing chess, of course. I find that it helps me relax and think strategically.
That makes sense. It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that your work as a documentary filmmaker gives you a lot of opportunities to travel?
Yeah, I do. I've been to a lot of different countries, including Japan, where I learned about origami. I'm actually based in Lisbon, Portugal right now.
I love the culture and history here. I've been taking care of my aging parents, who live with me, so it's been a bit of a challenge to balance work and family responsibilities.
It can be tough to balance everything. I hope everything works out for you and your parents. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the year?
Yeah, I'm actually planning a trip to the US in June to attend a film festival and promote my documentary.
That sounds like a great opportunity. I'm sure your documentary will be a hit. Good luck with everything.
Thanks, I appreciate it. I'm just excited to share my passion with others and inspire people to learn more about chess and strategy.
I'm sure you will inspire people. You have a lot of knowledge and experience to share.
